arts.ttl: Gazzetta: Douglas Luiz on the market after a troubling start at Juventus
arts.sum: Douglas Luiz, the Brazilian midfielder who joined Juventus in a high-profile transfer from Aston Villa, is reportedly already on the market as the January transfer window approaches. Since his arrival, Luiz has struggled to make an impact, leading to speculation about his future with the club. La Gazzetta dello Sport report how the 26-year-old has been sidelined since October 22 due to injury and has failed to impress under the management of Thiago Motta. The anticipated chemistry between player and coach has not materialized, and Luiz’s performances have raised doubts about his suitability for Motta’s tactical approach. arts.ttl: Milan ordered to compensate Boban for wrongful dismissal
arts.sum: AC Milan has been ordered to compensate Zvonimir Boban following a legal battle over his dismissal from the club. The Croatian executive had appealed to the Court of Cassation against his termination for just cause, which has now been declared “illegitimate.” As a result, Milan must pay Boban 4.825 million euros, an amount the club had already accounted for in its financial statements. Calciomercato report how the legal journey for Boban began with a victory in the first instance, followed by an appeal that favored Milan. Initially, the former player was supposed to return 1.25 million euros to the club, The post Milan ordered to compensate Boban for wrongful dismissal appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: COACHES ON THEIR DEBUT IN AC MILAN VS. JUVENTUS
arts.sum: The match scheduled for Saturday 23 at 18:00 CET at the Meazza represents an historical anomaly: Juventus (sixth) and AC Milan (seventh) face off in the league at a time when they both find themselves outside of the top-five in the Serie A since the first day of the season, something which has just happened once in the last 50 years, precisely on 21 November 2015, when the bianconeri won 1-0 thanks to a goal from Paulo Dybala. In five previous meetings, however, Motta has had the better of the rossoneri on just one occasion, as he led Spezia at San Siro, a stadium where the current bianconeri manager has not lost on two occasions against AC Milan.
